question: What's the best time to visit Port Macquarie NSW with kids?
answer: You can't go wrong visiting Port Macquarie year-round, mate! September to November is absolutely bonzer – warm weather, fewer crowds, and cheaper rates. School holidays (especially summer) are buzzing but pricier. Winter's still lovely with mild temps around 20°C, perfect for beach walks and wildlife spotting. Avoid peak summer if you're not keen on crowds, but the kids will love it regardless!

question: Are there pet-friendly holiday parks in Port Macquarie NSW?
answer: Too right there are! Many Port Macquarie holiday parks welcome your furry mates. BIG4 Sundowner, Flynns Beach Resort, and Settlement City all have pet-friendly cabins and sites. You'll usually need to book specific pet-approved accommodation and keep dogs on leads in common areas. Most charge a small pet fee. Always ring ahead to confirm their pet policy and availability, especially during busy periods.

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Port Macquarie NSW during school holidays?
answer: During school holidays, expect to pay $150-$350+ per night for cabins depending on size and facilities. Powered sites run $50-$80 per night. Peak Christmas holidays are the priciest. You'll save heaps visiting off-peak – sometimes 30-40% less! Most parks have minimum stay requirements during holidays (usually 3-7 nights). Book early for the best deals and availability, trust me on this one!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Essentials: swimmers, sunscreen (SPF50+), hats, beach toys, and insect repellent. Pack comfort items like favourite toys and bedtime books. Bring your own towels (many parks charge for them), and basic groceries to save cash. Don't forget kids' paracetamol, bandaids, and any medications. A portable highchair's handy if you've got littlies. Most cabins have kitchens, so BYO basics!

question: What is there to do near Port Macquarie NSW holiday parks for families?
answer: Heaps to keep the little ones entertained! Visit the Koala Hospital (it's free!), explore gorgeous beaches like Flynn's and Town Beach, or try Billabong Zoo. The Coastal Walk's perfect for prams. Kids love Sea Acres Rainforest Centre and the Timbertown heritage park. There's mini golf, playgrounds, and river cruises too. Many holiday parks have their own pools and jumping pillows – easy!
